title: Unemployment
description: Practice what you've learned about unemployment, including how to calculate the unemployment rate, in this exercise.
topic_path: Economic indicators and the business cycle

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows employment data for the country of Gerbilville.

Status | # of people 
- | - 
Adult population | $1,000$
Employed | $600$
Not working but seeking employment | $150$
Not working and not seeking employment | $250$ 

**Which of the following calculations correctly determines the unemployment rate in Gerbilville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**If unemployed workers decide to stop looking for a job and instead enroll in further education, how will the number of employed workers and the unemployment rate change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The number of employed workers does not change; the unemployment rate decreases
The number of employed workers does not change; the unemployment rate does not change
The number of employed workers increases; the unemployment rate decreases
The number of employed workers decreases; the unemployment rate increases
The number of employed workers does not change; the unemployment rate increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the correct interpretation of an unemployment rate of 4%?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: 4% of the labor force is out of work and looking for work.
4% of the population is looking for work.
4% of the population is out of work and looking for work.
Four percent of the population is in the labor force.
Four percent of the population has given up looking for work.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows population and employment data for Downtown Abbey, a country with a total population of $1$ million people.

Status | # of people 
- | - 
Children $0\text{-}15$ years old | $200,000$
Employed | $325,000$
Unemployed | $25,000$
Discouraged adult workers | $50,000$
Full-time students over $15$ | $100,000$
Retired individuals | $100,000$
Stay-at-home parents and other non-working civilians over $16$ | $100,000$
Non-civilians (employed by armed forces) | $60,000$
Institutionalized individuals | $40,000$


**What is Downtown Abbey's labor force participation rate (LFPR)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The labor force participation rate counts the percentage of the eligible population (adult, civilian, and non-institutionalized people) that are either employed or unemployed. 

$\text{LFPR}=\dfrac{\text{unemployed}+\text{employed}}{\text{eligible population}}$

First determine the eligible population in Downton Abbey. This includes adults who are not institutionalized and not in the armed forces. 

There are $800,000$ people over the age of $16$. Once we subtract the $100,000$ people either in the armed forces or in an institution, this leaves an eligible population of $700,000$ people.

Finally, we must divide the number of employed and unemployed by the eligible population to find the LFPR.

$\dfrac{325,000+25,000}{700,000}\times100=50\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would most likely cause a nation's labor force participation rate (LFPR) to increase?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Employed workers lose their jobs and become unemployed
Paid workers leave their jobs to focus on full-time volunteer work instead of paid work
Fewer parents choose to stay at home with their children and instead take part-time jobs
There is an increase in the number of people leaving jobs and going to college full-time
The retirement age is lowered, which leads more people to retire earlier

assessment_items.question_content:
There are 125,000 people in Hamiltonia. Of these, 25,000 are children. There are 50,000 people employed and 30,000 people unemployed. 

**What is the labor force participation rate in Hamiltonia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To calculate the labor force participation rate (LFPR), use the following equation:
$LFPR=(\dfrac{\#\text{ in labor force}}{\#\text{ in eligible population}}) \times 100\%$

To be in the labor force, a person must either be employed or unemployed.
There are 50,000 people employed and 30,000 people unemployed. Therefore, the size of the labor force is 80,000:

$50{,}000 + 30{,}000 = 80{,}000$
The eligible population includes only adults. Of the 125,000 people in Hamiltonia, 25,000 are children. Therefore the eligible population is 100,000:

$125{,}000 - 25{,}000 = 100{,}000$
The labor force participation rate in Hamiltonia is 80%:

$\begin{align}LFPR &=\dfrac{80{,}000}{100{,}000} \times 100\%\\\\
&=0.8 \times 100\%\\\\
&=80\%\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the labor force participation rate measure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The percentage of the eligible population that is either employed or out of work and searching for work
The percent of the labor force that is unemployed
The percent of the population that has given up looking for work
The percentage of people who are looking for work
The number of people out of the labor force

assessment_items.question_content:
Sonja was out of a job for a year. She recently gave up looking for work because there were no jobs available.

**Which of the following best describes the effect of Sonja exiting the labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The unemployment rate decreases
The unemployment rate increases
The unemployment rate will not change
The labor force participation rate increases
The size of the eligible population decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the unemployment rate falls when someone who is unemployed stops looking for work?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The number of people unemployed decreases; the size of the labor force decreases
The number of people unemployed decreases; the labor force increases
The number of people unemployed increases; the labor force decreases
The number of people unemployed increases; the labor force increases
The number of people unemployed decreases; the labor force doesn't change

assessment_items.question_content:
Burgin works part-time serving bubble tea at a Karaoke club while he tries to find a full-time job as a lumberjack.

**Which of the following best describes Burgin's employment situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: underemployed
cyclically unemployed
structurally unemployed
frictionally unemployed
discouraged worker

assessment_items.question_content:
Laurens quit his job as a scientist to study full time to be a lawyer. 

**Which of the following best describes Laurens’ employment status?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Not in the labor force
Structurally unemployed
Frictionally unemployed
Cyclically unemployed
Employed

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a reason that the official unemployment rate can understate the actual unemployment situation in an economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Discouraged workers are not in the labor force.
Unemployed workers looking for work are not in the labor force.
Noncitizens are not counted in the labor force.
Part-time workers are not counted as employed.
Underemployed workers are counted as unemployed.
